Protein families: Class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily, Cation-independent O-methyltransferase family, COMT subfamily
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily. Cation-independent O-methyltransferase family. COMT subfamily. {ECO:0000255|PROSITE-ProRule:PRU01020}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=anthranilate + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = N-methylanthranilate + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:12180, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:16567, ChEBI:CHEBI:36557, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789; EC=2.1.1.111; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:17988223};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Involved in the biosynthesis of acridine alkaloids. N-methyltransferase with a strict substrate specificity for anthranilate. No activity with anthranilic acid methyl ester, anthraniloyl CoA, 3- or 4-amino-benzoic acid, salicylic acid, catechol, eugenol, caffeic acid, quercetin, theobromin, theophyllin, putrescine and nicotinic acid among others. {ECO:0000269|PubMed:17988223}.
